hypothesis: E630 flagged (but explicitly did not fix, calling it a different-harness deferral) that `_is_meaningful_program`'s literal `"Card([])"` substring check misses an empty-children component whose remaining (non-content) positional arguments got padded with stuffed required-slot values -- e.g. `Card([], ":a", ":b")` -- because the substring only matches an *exactly*-empty call. This iteration verifies that gap against real code and real data (not just E630's paraphrase), and if genuine, fixes it narrowly without weakening any existing rejection.
which_deferral_this_picks: E630's own 'Next step' #3: '_is_meaningful_program's literal "Card([])" / "Stack([])" substring checks miss an empty-children component padded with trailing non-content properties -- observed evidence here, not fixed (different harness, evals.meaningful_program, out of this iteration's scope), worth hardening to a structural emptiness check in a future iteration.' (Note: the function actually lives in `harnesses/model_build/eval_runner.py`, not `evals/meaningful_program.py` -- E630's own text named the wrong module; `evals/meaningful_program.py` is the separate `binding_aware_meaningful_v2` metric and has no literal empty-substring check at all, confirmed by inspection, no change needed there.)

verification_before_fixing.method: Read the real implementation (src/slm_training/harnesses/model_build/eval_runner.py::_is_meaningful_program, public alias meaningful_program_v1) instead of trusting E630's prose paraphrase, then parsed the exact E629/E630-reported padded strings through the real DSL parser (slm_training.dsl.parser.validate) to inspect Program.root, the actual AST.
verification_before_fixing.finding: Confirmed exactly as E630 described, with one added precision: the literal checks are `if "Stack([])" in compact or "Stack([]," in compact: ... elif "Card([])" in compact: ...` -- Stack already has a companion `"Stack([],"` clause for the padded/trailing-args case, but Card does not. So the gap is not just 'one syntactic shape' in the abstract -- it is an asymmetry already visible in the code: Stack's own literal check already handles this exact padded shape, Card's does not.
verification_before_fixing.ast_shape_confirmed: Program.root for `Card([], ":a", ":b")` is `{type: element, typeName: Card, props: {children: [], variant: ":a", direction: ":b"}}` -- `children` is a key in `props`, structurally separate from the other (stuffed) prop values, and independently inspectable regardless of what the other props hold.
verification_before_fixing.schema_scope_check: Queried src/slm_training/dsl/grammars/openui_schema.json directly: four component types declare a required `children` array property -- Card, Carousel, Stack, Modal. The old literal check only ever special-cased Stack and Card by name; Carousel and Modal were never covered by any empty-check at all (confirmed: `root = Modal(":t", true, [])` and `root = Carousel([])` both passed meaningful_program_v1 pre-fix).

fix.location: src/slm_training/harnesses/model_build/eval_runner.py -- new `_first_empty_children_component(node)` helper, called from `_is_meaningful_program` immediately after (not instead of) the existing literal Stack/Card checks.
fix.design: Recursively walks the real parsed AST (`Program.root`, an ElementNode tree with `props` dicts and `props.children` arrays -- not the serialized text). For any element node whose `props['children']` is a list and is empty, returns that node's `typeName`. Reads `children` from wherever the parser itself put it (a dict key inside `props`), so it is inherently immune to stuffing extra values into a component's *other* props -- there is no serialized-text shape to dodge, because the check never looks at the text.
fix.why_purely_additive_not_a_gate_weakening: The two pre-existing literal branches (`Stack([])`/`Stack([],`/`Card([])`) are left completely unchanged and still run first, with their exact original reason strings. The new AST check only runs after them and can only ADD new rejections (any empty-children node the literal checks miss); it can never cause a program the literal checks already rejected to now pass, because that branch still returns False first, before the AST check is ever reached. This satisfies the iron-law requirement that a scoring-metric change must make the check strictly more correct, never looser.
fix.backward_compatible_reason_strings: When the AST check fires on a `Stack` or `Card` node, it returns the exact same reason strings the literal checks already used (`empty_root_stack`, `empty_card`) -- so no existing consumer of those reason strings (dashboards, failure_breakdown bucketing, tests) sees a new vocabulary for the two cases E630 already knew about. Only genuinely new component types (Modal, Carousel) get a new reason code, `empty_children:<TypeName>`.
fix.diagnostics_bucketing: src/slm_training/harnesses/model_build/decode_feasibility.py::classify_parse_failure additionally buckets any `empty_children:*` reason into the existing `trivial_layout` diagnostic bucket (alongside `empty_root_stack`/`empty_card`/`no_content_components`) -- a diagnostics-only change, does not affect meaningful_program_v1's pass/fail outcome.

new_parametrized_cases: root = Card([], ":stuffed.variant") -> empty_card (the exact rico_eval_test_25 shape)
root = Card([], ":ood.dash.m1.value", ":ood.dash.m1.value") -> empty_card (the exact ood_dashboard_01 shape)
nested Stack([Card(non-empty), Card([], stuffed)]) -> empty_card (nesting does not evade the check)
root = Modal(":title", true, []) -> empty_children:Modal (new coverage, previously undetected)
root = Carousel([]) -> empty_children:Carousel (new coverage, previously undetected)
regression_guards: root = Card([TextContent(":a")], ":real.variant") -> True (a genuinely non-empty Card with other props present must still pass)
root = Stack([Card([TextContent(":a")])], "column") -> True (nested genuinely non-empty must still pass)
tests.existing_backward_lock_test_unmodified: test_meaningful_program_v1_backward_lock (Stack([])->empty_root_stack, Card([])->empty_card, populated Stack->None, Separator->no_placeholders) still passes unchanged -- confirms the literal-check cases are bit-for-bit preserved.
tests.result: 36/36 passed in tests/test_evals/test_meaningful_program.py (29 pre-existing + 7 new); 56/56 passed across test_audit_meaningful_program.py, test_matrix_meaningful_metrics.py, test_decode_invariance.py, test_eval_gates.py, test_language_contract.py.

checkpoint_reused.note: E626's own scratch checkpoint reused verbatim (no retrain, no new decode-time code touched this session -- E630's twotower.py schema-position-gate fix stays exactly as committed); sha256 verified to match E626/E628/E629/E630's own JSON before running.

honest_re_scoring_against_real_checkpoin.purpose: Re-run the same recipe E629/E630 used, with only the metric code changed (the fixed meaningful_program_v1), to see whether any previously-passing record's judged status flips now that the check is stricter -- required by this task, not skipped.
honest_re_scoring_against_real_checkpoin.recipe: python -m scripts.evaluate_model --checkpoint outputs/runs/e626-required-slot-margin-scratch800-20260720/checkpoints/last.pt --model twotower --test-dir src/slm_training/resources/data/eval/remediated --suites held_out,rico_held,adversarial,ood,smoke --honest-slot-contract --slot-contract-constrained-decode --slot-contract-in-context --semantic-role-contract-in-context --semantic-role-decode-weight 8 --slot-coverage-close-decode-weight 2 --schema-value-decode-weight 4 --schema-opaque-close-decode-weight 4 --schema-role-slot-decode-weight 8 --semantic-plan-decode-weight 4 --semantic-plan-margin-decode-weight 2 --semantic-plan-binding-decode-weight 1 --semantic-plan-root-decode-weight 8 --semantic-plan-root-margin-decode-weight 2 --semantic-plan-repeated-array-close-margin-decode-weight 2 --semantic-plan-repeated-slot-margin-decode-weight 2 --semantic-plan-typed-array-nonempty-margin-decode-weight 2 --semantic-plan-typed-array-item-margin-decode-weight 2 --grammar-ltr-max-tokens 160 --required-slot-margin-decode-weight <0|2> --run-id e631-verify-margin<X> --run-root outputs/runs

pooled_n19_margin0_control_before_vs_aft.flip_source: rico_held alone: 1.0 (3/3) before -> 0.3333 (1/3) after, at margin=0 -- the pure baseline decode, with required_slot_margin_decode_weight=0 (the lever entirely inactive). held_out/adversarial/ood/smoke rates are unchanged at margin=0 (0.6/0.5/0.5/1.0 respectively, matching E629 exactly).

which_records_flipped_and_why.method: Monkeypatched _is_meaningful_program in-process to print (record_id, ok, reason, serialized_prediction) while re-running the real evaluate_model CLI against the same checkpoint/suite/recipe -- direct observation of the actual model output, not inference from aggregate numbers.

rico_eval_test_42.note: Genuinely new catch, correct and expected: v5/v6/v9 are all `Card([], <stuffed-non-content-value>)` -- an empty-children Card whose `variant`-position argument absorbed an unrelated value. This is present in the checkpoint's UNMODIFIED baseline decode (required_slot_margin_decode_weight=0, the lever entirely off) -- so this over-stuffing artifact was never specific to the lever under test at all; it is a pre-existing gap in the checkpoint's own output that the old literal check happened to miss regardless of margin.

held_out.note: unchanged binary rate vs margin0; one record's continuous metrics differ (reward 0.7682->0.642, structural_similarity 0.301->0.274) -- a cosmetic margin-sensitive prediction swap that does not cross the meaningful gate, matching the kind of cosmetic diff E629 already documented elsewhere in this suite.

rico_held.note: Byte-identical predictions and metrics between margin=0 and margin=2 (reward=0.897, fidelity=0.6667 both) -- rico_eval_test_25 stays fixed (E630's decode fix holds), and rico_eval_test_42/77 are equally (correctly) rejected at both margins, since their empty-Card padding is unrelated to required_slot_margin entirely.

ood.note: byte-identical between margin0/margin2 -- confirms E630's own finding that ood_dashboard_01's margin=2 gain is fully reverted by E630's decode fix, and the metric fix changes nothing further here (no Modal/Carousel present in this suite's predictions).

smoke.note: drops from 1.0 (margin0) to 0.6667 (margin2) -- this is smoke_hero_01's previously-documented (E630) regression, driven entirely by the decode bias itself (empty_root_stack), not by this session's metric change; unaffected by the metric fix.

honest_re_scoring_against_real_checkpoin.net_conclusion_under_the_corrected_metri: The corrected metric shifts both the margin=0 and margin>=1 baselines down by exactly the same 2 records (rico_eval_test_42/77, unrelated to the lever, present at every margin equally) -- so the *delta* between margin=0 and margin>=1 (the quantity that actually matters for judging required_slot_margin_decode_weight) is essentially unchanged in direction: still net negative post-E630-fix (10/19 at margin=2 vs 11/19 at margin=0, i.e. -1, matching E630's own pre-metric-fix conclusion of -1, 12/19 vs 13/19). The metric fix does not change E630's headline verdict about the lever (still: fixed regression, reverted gain, net non-positive on this checkpoint) -- it only corrects the absolute baseline level by catching two real, pre-existing, lever-independent scoring artifacts.

verdict: E630's diagnosis was confirmed accurate against real code and real data, with one added precision (Stack's literal check already had the padded-args companion clause that Card's lacked). The fix is a purely additive AST-structural check that cannot remove any existing rejection, verified with dedicated regression tests for the newly-caught case, the two previously-caught cases (unchanged reason strings), and non-empty pass-through cases. Re-scoring the actual E626 scratch checkpoint's real predictions (not a synthetic replay) confirms two previously (incorrectly) passing rico_held records now correctly fail, at every margin including the pure control -- an expected, honest tightening, not a regression to hide. The corrected metric leaves E630's own net-effect conclusion about required_slot_margin_decode_weight intact (still net non-positive on this checkpoint/suite): both flipped records fail identically regardless of margin, so they cancel out of the before/after delta and only shift the absolute baseline.
what_this_is_not: A single scratch checkpoint (800 steps, seed 0); n=19 (widened suite, still below DEFAULT_MIN_SUITE_N=20 per suite) -- not a ship claim, not a powered multi-seed result. Does not retrain or resweep margins 3/4 (already shown by E629/E630 to add nothing over 1/2). Does not touch evals/meaningful_program.py (binding_aware_meaningful_v2 / strict v2), which has no literal empty-substring check of this kind at all -- confirmed by direct inspection, not just deferred.
next_step: 1) E620's original coverage-aware component/property closure recommendation remains the next lever this lineage has not directly tried. 2) A genuinely powered multi-seed/retrained comparison of required_slot_margin_decode_weight remains open (E626's own deferral, still not picked up by E627-E631). 3) smoke_hero_01's regression (first surfaced in E630, unaffected by this session's metric fix) remains untraced.
